name: La Cervatina – running circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28)
name_original: La Cervatina – running circular desde Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28)
region_intro: Jogging around Puebla De Lillo offers diverse terrain within the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park, characterized by a majestic mountain environment, verdant valleys, and the Porma River. The region features varying elevations, from gentle slopes along riverbanks to more challenging uphill routes, making it suitable for different intensities of running. Key natural features include the Pinar de Lillo, glacial lakes like Lake Isoba and Lake Ausente, and numerous streams. This landscape provides a range of jogging routes, from serene forest paths to invigorating mountain trails. Best jogging routes around Puebla De Lillo The most popular jogging route is La Cervatina – running circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28), a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path through the natural surroundings of Puebla de Lillo. Another top favourite among local runners is Lake Ausente loop from Parking de Cebolledo, a difficult 4.0 miles (6.5 km) path. This trail provides scenic views around the glacial Lake Ausente, offering an invigorating run in a high-altitude setting. Local runners also love the Susarón – El Torreón loop from Puebla de Lillo, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 1 hour 45 minutes. Jogging around Puebla De Lillo is defined by its majestic mountain environment, the Porma River, and serene glacial lakes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain ascents to more moderate forest and riverside paths. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 220 runners have used komoot to explore Puebla De Lillo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around Puebla de Lillo?
A: The running routes in Puebla de Lillo, located within the Montaña de Riaño y Mampodre Regional Park, offer a diverse terrain. You'll find everything from gentle slopes along riverbanks, particularly near the Porma River, to more challenging uphill routes through its majestic mountain environment. The region is characterized by verdant valleys, forests like the Pinar de Lillo, and serene glacial lakes, providing varied surfaces and elevations for all types of runners.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate jogging routes available in Puebla de Lillo?
A: While many of the trails in Puebla de Lillo are rated as difficult due to the mountainous terrain, there is at least one moderate route available. For those seeking a less strenuous run, consider paths along riverbanks or around the glacial lakes, which tend to offer flatter sections. The region's diverse landscape means you can often find gentler slopes even within more challenging areas.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Puebla de Lillo area?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular running routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055942014/la-cervatina-running-circular-from-puebla-de-lillo-pr-le-28" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cervatina – running circular from Puebla de Lillo (PR-LE 28)&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 9.3-mile (15.0 km) trail offering a complete loop through the natural surroundings. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24568328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón – El Torreón loop from Puebla de Lillo&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.6-mile (9.0 km) route that takes you through mountainous terrain.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or natural attractions I can see while jogging in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Puebla de Lillo is rich in natural beauty. You can enjoy stunning views of the surrounding mountains, including peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yordas (1,967 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5543307" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón Peak (1,878 m)&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features the serene Lake Isoba and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147084" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Ausente&lt;/a&gt;, which offers invigorating runs with scenic vistas. Don't miss the Pinar de Lillo, a rare indigenous pine forest, for a unique and tranquil running experience.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer river views in Puebla de Lillo?
A: Absolutely. The Porma River and its tributaries flow through the region, providing numerous opportunities for runs with beautiful river views. These paths often feature flat to gently sloping terrain, accompanied by the soothing sounds of flowing water, making for a very pleasant jogging experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The mild summers in Puebla de Lillo are ideal for outdoor activities like jogging. During this season, the weather is pleasant, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. However, the changing seasons also offer unique beauty, with wildflowers in spring and golden-red foliage in autumn, making these times also very appealing for runners.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Puebla de Lillo?
A: While many routes are challenging, the region offers paths suitable for families. Look for trails along the Porma River or around Lake Isoba, which tend to be less steep and provide a more relaxed environment. These areas are perfect for a family jog or a leisurely run, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural surroundings.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging experience in Puebla de Lillo?
A: The komoot community highly values the running experience in Puebla de Lillo. Over 220 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the majestic mountain environment, the serene glacial lakes, and the diverse network of trails that cater to different ability levels. The region's natural beauty and well-maintained paths are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any specific routes that showcase the unique forests of Puebla de Lillo?
A: Yes, the Pinar de Lillo is a standout feature. This rare indigenous pine forest, located between Puebla de Lillo and Puerto de las Señales, offers a unique and scenic environment for jogging. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24567811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cofiñal Rest Area – Tronisco Beech Forest loop from Area Recreativa de Cofiñal&lt;/a&gt; for a beautiful run through a beech forest.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Puebla de Lillo area?
A: There are over a dozen dedicated running routes around Puebla de Lillo documented on komoot. These routes vary in length and difficulty, ensuring there's a suitable option whether you're looking for a moderate jog or a challenging mountain run.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The protected environment of Puebla de Lillo is rich in wildlife. While jogging, you might have the chance to spot deer, wild boars, foxes, badgers, and various bird species. The diverse flora and fauna add to the immersive natural experience of running in this region.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: For experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24568328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Susarón – El Torreón loop from Puebla de Lillo&lt;/a&gt;, with its significant elevation gain, offer an invigorating workout. The trails around the base or lower sections of Pico Yordas also provide demanding paths with rewarding panoramic views of the surrounding mountains.
tips: Beautiful views of the town of Puebla de Lillo when we walk along the picturesque path PR LE-28 of the Ruta de la Cervatina next to the Silvan river.
Nice green area where we will find picnic tables. An ideal place to take a break in a fantastic setting.
